paper_title: Material and Methods of Fossil Analysis

methods.description: Process for measuring fossil specimens using sliding calipers to ensure accuracy and consistency in data collection.

steps.description: Measurements were made with sliding calipers to an accuracy of 0.1 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: measure fossils using sliding calipers with 0.1 mm accuracy

steps.description: Adjust the length measurement for teeth that have undergone interstitial wear to account for the missing elements.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust tooth length measurements for wear

methods.description: Visual assessment of enamel thickness for classifying teeth as thin-enamelled or thick-enamelled.

steps.description: Visual assessments were made for some teeth classing them as thin-enamelled (ca. 1 mm or less) or thick-enamelled (more than 1.5 mm).
steps.searchable_summary: classify teeth based on visual enamel thickness assessment

methods.description: Collection of geographic coordinates using GPS for fossil localities.

steps.description: Geographic co-ordinates were obtained with GPS set to WGS 84 datum.
steps.searchable_summary: collect geographic coordinates using GPS with WGS 84 datum

methods.description: System for numbering fossiliferous localities in the Tugen Hills to avoid confusion.

steps.description: From 1972 to 1981, fossiliferous localities in the Tugen Hills were numbered with prefix 1/** and 2/** for the localities south and north of latitude 0°40′ N respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: number fossil localities in Tugen Hills with prefix system

steps.description: In 1982 an alternative system was put in place by which all the localities were renumbered and given the prefix BPRP.
